assertion_results.text: Test Quality verdict is WARN (not N/A or PASS) — the Eval Quality component is WARN because eval-3 has 2 failing assertions, and WARN in any component produces WARN in the combined Test Quality verdict
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 13: '| Test Quality | WARN | Eval Quality: WARN -- eval-3 has 2 failing assertions (85% pass rate); per-eval rates: eval-1 100%, eval-2 100%, eval-3 85% (11/13), eval-4 100%, eval-5 100%; overall pass rate 91%; 2 regression failures in eval-3 triggered sub-task creation |'. The Test Quality verdict is WARN, driven by the Eval Quality component being WARN due to eval-3's 2 failing assertions.

assertion_results.text: The eval result review from github-actions[bot] is correctly identified as an eval result — the report's Test Quality or Eval Quality section reflects eval metrics (pass rates, failing assertions) extracted from the eval review, confirming the orchestrator detected and processed it
assertion_results.evidence: report.md lines 29-34 contain an 'Eval Result Detection' section identifying Review ID 40001 from github-actions[bot] with three detection criteria met: '(1) author is github-actions[bot], (2) body contains ## Eval Results marker, (3) body contains sdlc-workflow/run-evals footer'. Lines 38-44 contain an 'Eval Metrics' table with per-eval pass rates. Lines 132-133 confirm 'Eval Quality -- WARN' in the Style/Conventions Analysis section. The eval review was detected and processed into Test Quality metrics.

assertion_results.text: The human reviewer comment from reviewer-b (comment id 50001) is NOT misidentified as an eval result — it is processed as a normal review comment (classified in review-50001.md) and its classification does not reference eval detection or eval metrics
assertion_results.evidence: review-50001.md exists and classifies comment 50001. Line 5: 'Type: Inline review comment (NOT an eval result review)'. Line 7: 'Author: reviewer-b (human reviewer)'. Line 12: 'Classification: Code Change Request'. Lines 14-16 explicitly state: 'This is a human reviewer comment from reviewer-b... It is NOT an eval result review -- it does not meet the three-criteria eval detection test'. The classification reasoning discusses directive language and review context, with no reference to eval metrics or eval detection.

assertion_results.text: Eval pass rate and failing assertion details appear in the verification report's Test Quality row — the report includes eval-3's failing assertions about convention upgrade eligibility and sub-task creation
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 13 Test Quality row includes '85% pass rate' and 'overall pass rate 91%'. Lines 48-56 detail the two failing assertions: (1) 'Convention upgrade eligibility is evaluated for review comment 30002 (index suggestion)' with evidence about missing CONVENTIONS.md lookup, and (2) 'Review comment 30002 (index suggestion) results in a sub-task regardless of classification path' with evidence about no sub-task being created. Both are about convention upgrade eligibility and sub-task creation as required.

assertion_results.text: An eval failure sub-task is created for failing eval-3 — a sub-task description file targets eval-3 assertion failures and includes the failing assertion text and evidence
assertion_results.evidence: subtask-1.md line 8: 'Fix eval-3 assertion failures related to convention upgrade eligibility evaluation and sub-task creation for review comments classified as suggestions.' Lines 22-28 include the Review Context section with both failing assertion texts and their evidence. Assertion 1 text quotes 'Convention upgrade eligibility is evaluated for review comment 30002' with evidence about missing convention analysis. Assertion 2 text quotes 'Review comment 30002 (index suggestion) results in a sub-task' with evidence about no sub-task creation.

assertion_results.text: Sub-task descriptions include Review Context with the failing assertion text and evidence from the eval review — the Review Context section quotes the two failing assertions about convention upgrade eligibility and sub-task creation, including the evidence text explaining what was missing
assertion_results.evidence: subtask-1.md lines 20-28: '## Review Context' section contains two assertion blocks. Assertion 1 Text: 'Convention upgrade eligibility is evaluated for review comment 30002 (index suggestion)...' with Evidence: 'The output file review-30002.md classifies the comment as a suggestion but does not evaluate convention upgrade eligibility -- no CONVENTIONS.md lookup or codebase pattern analysis is documented in the classification reasoning'. Assertion 2 Text: 'Review comment 30002 (index suggestion) results in a sub-task regardless of classification path...' with Evidence: 'No sub-task was created for review comment 30002 -- it was classified as suggestion and no convention upgrade was attempted, so the suggestion was not elevated to a code change request'. Both assertions include full evidence text explaining what was missing.

assertion_results.text: Sub-task descriptions include a Target PR section pointing to the PR URL https://github.com/RHEcosystemAppEng/sdlc-plugins/pull/747 so that implement-task adds commits to the existing PR branch
assertion_results.evidence: subtask-1.md line 30-31: '## Target PR' followed by 'https://github.com/RHEcosystemAppEng/sdlc-plugins/pull/747'. subtask-2.md line 19-20: '## Target PR' followed by 'https://github.com/RHEcosystemAppEng/sdlc-plugins/pull/747'. Both sub-task description files include the Target PR section with the correct URL.

assertion_results.text: Root-cause investigation runs on the created eval failure sub-tasks — the report includes a Root-Cause Investigation verdict that is not N/A, indicating the investigation pipeline processed the eval failure sub-tasks
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 6: '| Root-Cause Investigation | DONE | Eval failure sub-tasks investigated; convention upgrade pipeline gap identified as implement-task skill gap |'. The verdict is DONE (not N/A). Lines 73-84 provide detailed Root-Cause Investigation content including universality test ('applies to ANY repository'), phase attribution ('implement-task phase'), and classification ('Skill gap -- the implement-task phase did not fully implement the convention upgrade evaluation pipeline').

assertion_results.text: The skill does NOT modify any code files — it only reads, verifies, creates sub-task descriptions, and reports (constraint 1.11)
assertion_results.evidence: The outputs directory contains only analysis and description files: report.md (verification report), review-50001.md (review comment classification), subtask-1.md and subtask-2.md (sub-task descriptions), and criterion-1.md through criterion-7.md (acceptance criteria evaluations). No source code files (.ts, .js, .py, .rs, .go, etc.) or skill definition files (style-conventions.md, SKILL.md) appear in the outputs. The report's Scope Containment section (line 90) confirms PR files were only compared against task specification, not modified. All outputs are read-only artifacts: classifications, descriptions, and reports.

assertion_results.text: Non-eval checks (Scope Containment, Sensitive Patterns, Acceptance Criteria) are unaffected by eval processing — Scope Containment compares the diff files against the task's Files to Modify, Sensitive Patterns scans the diff for secrets, and Acceptance Criteria evaluates each criterion against the diff content independently of the eval results
assertion_results.evidence: report.md lines 89-91 Scope Containment PASS: 'PR files match task specification exactly. Both plugins/sdlc-workflow/skills/verify-pr/style-conventions.md and plugins/sdlc-workflow/skills/verify-pr/SKILL.md are listed in the task's Files to Modify section and are the only files changed' -- compares diff files against task spec with no eval reference. Lines 99-101 Sensitive Patterns PASS: 'No sensitive patterns detected. The PR adds only documentation and instruction text in Markdown files. No passwords, API keys, tokens...' -- scans diff for secrets with no eval reference. Lines 108-117 Acceptance Criteria PASS: evaluates all 7 criteria individually against diff content (e.g., 'Check 6 scans for new public symbol definitions (step 6a) -- PASS'). criterion-1.md through criterion-7.md each evaluate a specific criterion against PR diff content with no reference to eval results. All three checks operate independently of eval processing.
